headline: GOLD HITS $4,812 — US-Iran Ceasefire Talks Drive Metals to Fresh 2026 High
snippet: Gold climbed to $4,812 per ounce on April 15, up $31 from the prior session and $1,472 above year-ago levels, as hopes for a second round of US-Iran peace talks pushed the dollar to a six-week low and pulled crude oil below $90. The metal is up more than 25% since early 2025 with analysts at ANZ projecting a run to $5,800 by Q2 2026.

headline: SILVER CROSSES $80 — Up 150% Year-Over-Year, Highest Level in Over a Decade
snippet: Spot silver approached $81 per ounce on April 15, more than 150% above year-ago levels and at its highest point in over a decade, fuelled by a surge in Chinese physical investment buying and solar-sector industrial demand. The gold-to-silver ratio, which peaked above 105 earlier this year, has contracted sharply toward 60 — a historically bullish technical pattern that has preceded prior silver outperformance cycles.

headline: GoldSeek Asian Metals Debrief: Gold, Silver, Copper Diverge as Dollar Drops
snippet: GoldSeek's April 14 Asian Metals Market Update covers intraday price action across gold, silver, copper, and platinum as Middle East tensions ease and the US dollar softens on Iran diplomacy news. The report highlights diverging momentum between safe-haven precious metals and industrial metals, and flags key near-term technical levels for active investors.

headline: NevGold Drills 0.34% ANTIMONY + 0.41 g/t Gold at Nevada Historic Leach Pads — Near-Term Production Path Emerging
snippet: NevGold Corp. (TSX-V: NAU) released April 14 sonic drill results from historic gold leach pads at its Limousine Butte project in Nevada, returning 0.34% antimony and 0.41 g/t gold over 12.5 metres in hole LBS26-001. The company is targeting a maiden antimony-gold resource estimate in Q2 2026 and near-term production by 2027 — a rare critical-mineral and near-surface production combination in a Tier 1 US jurisdiction.

headline: COPPER RECOVERS to $6.07/lb at Two-Month High — Iran Talks and Chile Permit Reform Boost Outlook
snippet: Copper futures climbed to $6.07 per pound on April 15 — equivalent to roughly $13,374 per tonne — fully recovering losses since the start of the Middle East conflict as Washington and Tehran prepare a second ceasefire round. Chile's new government has announced it will streamline permitting to accelerate mine output, adding a supply-side positive to an already tight market with J.P. Morgan projecting a 330,000-tonne refined copper deficit for 2026.

headline: GOLD'S HISTORIC FLIGHT: April 2026 Surge Signals Deepening Fiscal and Monetary Anxiety
snippet: This April 13 analysis argues that gold's sustained surge in 2026 reflects a structural shift from portfolio hedging to reserve asset substitution, as investors lose confidence in long-term fiscal and monetary stability in the US and Europe. The piece draws a distinction between trading gold as a hedge versus holding it as an alternative reserve — a distinction that matters enormously for how long and how far this bull market can extend.

headline: C$3 BILLION DEAL: G Mining Acquires G2 Goldfields — Junior M&A Season Is Back in Play
snippet: G Mining Ventures Corp. agreed to acquire G2 Goldfields in an all-share deal valued at approximately C$3 billion, combining adjacent Guyana gold assets into a district-scale complex targeting more than 500,000 oz/year and delivering over $1 billion in projected synergies. Analysts see the deal as a signal that the premium gold price environment is finally triggering the consolidation wave that junior investors have been waiting for.

headline: AbraSilver Drills 171m at 0.83 g/t Gold at DIABLILLOS — Oculto East Zone Expands Beyond Open Pit Resource
snippet: AbraSilver Resource Corp. (TSX-V: ABRA) intersected 171.0 metres grading 0.83 g/t gold and 10.3 g/t silver from 230 metres downhole at the Oculto East target within its Diablillos project in Argentina, with a high-grade section of 9 metres at 3.46 g/t gold confirming pods within the broad interval. The step-out result extends gold mineralisation beyond the current open-pit-constrained resource, adding resource expansion potential ahead of a Phase VI drill program.

headline: K92 MINING BEATS Q1 BUDGET — 46,743 AuEq oz Produced, Ore Throughput Up 37% YoY at Kainantu
snippet: K92 Mining (TSX: KNT) reported Q1 2026 production of 46,743 gold-equivalent ounces at its Kainantu Mine in Papua New Guinea, in line with budget and supported by a 37% year-over-year increase in ore throughput and record lateral development that now exceeds Stage 3 expansion requirements. The company maintained full-year 2026 guidance of 190,000–225,000 AuEq oz with the second half weighted heavier, and shares rose approximately 9.5% on the news.

headline: URANIUM HOLDS $85/lb — Active Spot Buying Signals Steady Demand Despite Middle East Volatility
snippet: Uranium spot delivered at $85.45 per pound U3O8 on April 14, up 32.7% year-over-year, as seven confirmed spot transactions totalling 500,000 pounds in a single week pointed to continued utility buying even amid broader market turbulence. Analysts note that with Brent crude above $100 per barrel, oil-fired power costs run roughly $185/MWh versus approximately $4.60/MWh for nuclear fuel — a spread that is accelerating long-term contracting commitments.

headline: US DEFICIT ADDICTION: $164B March Shortfall Halfway to $1.17 Trillion — Another Tailwind for Gold
snippet: The US government ran a $164.1 billion deficit in March 2026 — roughly 2% wider than March 2025 — pushing the fiscal year-to-date total to $1.17 trillion at the halfway mark, according to the Monthly Treasury Statement. Money Metals analysts note that structural deficit concerns have been a primary driver of central bank gold buying, and with no credible path to fiscal consolidation, the case for hard assets remains intact.
